## Preserved prior README notes

<!-- PRESERVED PRIOR README START -->
# pipeline_reports

This folder contains JSON reports emitted by the scoring scripts.

Reports are regenerated by running scripts in `../` with `--dry-run`.
They are meant to make the verdict derivation auditable without mutating
the released CSVs.

Typical command:

```powershell
cd 7.scoring
python .\add_test05_answer_verdict_columns.py --dry-run
```

## What Reports Contain

Depending on the surface, a report records:

- target CSV path,
- row count,
- verdict columns added or recomputed,
- changed-cell counts,
- distributions for each verdict column,
- gold values loaded from `../answer-key/answer_key.json`,
- the explicit if-and-only-if rule used by the script,
- whether the run was a dry run.

Schema B is report-only by design: the script reads the released
method-answer columns, computes strict correctness signals in memory,
and writes a report without adding columns to the CSV.

If this folder contains only `README.md`, run the scoring scripts with
`--dry-run` to regenerate the JSON reports.
<!-- PRESERVED PRIOR README END -->
